food_subgroup: Whole wheat flatbread stuffed with spiced potato

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has no specific concept for Aloo Paratha. Nearest parent is a stuffed flatbread. A new concept for Indian stuffed paratha preparations is recommended.
ontology.foodex2_description: Stuffed whole wheat flatbread (paratha) with potato filling (closest parent)

serving.natural_language_portion: A standard serving of aloo paratha is 1 piece (approximately 130g) — a round, golden-brown flatbread stuffed with spiced mashed potato, cooked on a griddle with ghee until crispy on both sides, served with yogurt and pickle.

nutrient_source.title: Aloo Paratha — HPB Singapore Foodo Database, Food Code F05020001; cross-referenced with ICMR-NIN
nutrient_source.organization: Health Promotion Board (HPB), Singapore; Indian Council of Medical Research — National Institute of Nutrition (ICMR-NIN)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Exact match in HPB Foodo under food code F05020001 — Aloo Paratha, standard Punjabi preparation with ghee.

gi_source: University of Sydney GI Database — estimated from whole wheat paratha with potato filling; the ghee and potato together give a moderate GI.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium approximately 406mg per piece (130g) — moderate; the ghee in cooking adds saturated fat but minimal sodium.
hypertension.guidance: Suitable in moderation for hypertensive patients. The high potassium from potato (370mg per paratha) partially offsets the sodium. Request oil-based cooking instead of ghee to reduce saturated fat.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate GI (~66); glycaemic load per piece (130g) is approximately 35 — high. The potato filling and whole wheat dough both contribute carbohydrates.
type2_diabetes.guidance: Diabetic patients should limit to half a piece and pair with a high-protein side (yogurt, dal) to moderate the glycaemic response. The ghee slightly lowers the GI but the total glycaemic load remains substantial.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Ghee provides butyrate and fat-soluble vitamins; whole wheat provides selenium and B vitamins; potato provides vitamin B6 and vitamin C.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Suitable in moderation for patients with rheumatoid arthritis who do not have gluten intolerance. The ghee and B vitamins provide some benefit. Nightshade-free diet followers should avoid (potato filling).

llm_training.natural_language_description: Aloo Paratha is the quintessential Punjabi breakfast and comfort food — a generous whole wheat flatbread stuffed with a spiced mashed potato filling (cumin, green chilli, coriander, amchur, garam masala) and cooked on a hot griddle with ghee until golden and slightly crispy on both sides. Rich, filling, and fragrant. Served with a bowl of cold fresh yogurt and a tangy pickle (achar). The combination of whole wheat dough and potato filling creates a complete, satisfying meal. In Singapore, aloo paratha is available at North Indian restaurants and some hawker stalls.

confidence_reason: Exact match found in HPB Singapore Foodo Database under food code F05020001 — Aloo Paratha, standard preparation.
notes: Contains gluten (wheat) and dairy (ghee). Not vegan or suitable for gluten-sensitive patients. Cholesterol approximately 23mg per piece (130g) from ghee. Vitamin C from potato and amchur. Some rheumatoid arthritis patients follow nightshade-free diets which would exclude the potato filling.
